rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ROBERT P. CLARKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/09/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) Immediately upon the occurrence of facts making it necessary or appropriate to increase the amount of the bond, the conservator of the estate must make an ex parte application for an order increasing bond (CRC 7.204(a)). 2) An account will not be approved until the additional bond is on file (LRSF 14.16.A.2). Please address the above issues and provide this examiner with courtesy copies of any additional filed documents no later than February 23, 2026, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT P. CLARKE REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 3/28/23
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/09/2026. Issue necessitating a continuance: The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period (LRSF 14.34.D). Please address the above issue and provide this examiner with courtesy copies of any additional filed documents no later than February 23, 2026, to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAIME LACANLALE ALAMARES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/9/26. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. The caption of the petition does not match the body. Please clarify whether authorization under IAEA is being requested. 2. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. Please revise Item 8 of Petition. 3. Notice required under Probate Code 8113 has not been provided. 4. Phone number in Duties and Liablities (DE-147) is missing. The form must be filed again.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEIL JUSTIN LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following defects: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Item 2 of proof of service attached to the DE-121 form was left blank (address of person signing proof of service). 3) Direct notice must be given to each minor heir, regardless of the minor's age, in PES cases. See CRC 7.51(d). If this hearing proceeds with the foregoing defects waived, the judicial officer is to review and determine: A) Should a guardian of the estate of both minor children of the decedent be required to allow the guardian, once appointed, to claim priority for his/her own appointment (or his/her nominee's) as administrator of this estate under Probate Code sections 8464 and 8465(a)(3)? Petitioner, the decedent's mother, has no priority under section 8462 because she is not a beneficiary. The proposed guardian ad litem for the decedent's minor children does not have the same priority given to the children or guardian of their estate. See Estate of Trego (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 530. Notice must be given to the Public Administrator as a person having higher priority unless the Court specifically authorizes the guardian ad litem to exercise the children's priority to nominate an administrator. B) If the proposed guardian ad litem should be appointed: a) Should the guadian ad litem be allowed to exercise the children's priority to nominate petitioner, or should the guardian ad litem be directed to nominate a neutral professional fiduciary? b) Should the guardian ad litem be given authority to waive bond? c) Should the guardian ad litem be required to obtain prior court approval to waive or disclaim any substantive rights of each minor, as set forth in paragraph 6 of the proposed order appointing the guardian ad litem, including a waiver of accountings? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
